rationale: Rubin (2004) is not cited as generic background; this note is explicitly structured as a response to discussants and includes Rubin as one of the three focal commentators. The authors directly address Rubin's methodological point about posterior predictive checks, explain how their MCMC holdout prediction relates to that suggestion, and concede his broader model-checking recommendation as a valuable future direction. This is clear functional engagement with a specific critique, so the citation is substantive.
relation_description: The citing paper explicitly responds to Rubin (2004) as a discussant on the authors' earlier conjoint model. It engages his recommendation on posterior predictive checks and uses it to clarify current validation practice and motivate additional model-assessment work.
